From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

English lawn bowler (born 1983)

Mark Dawes

| Personal information | |
| --- | --- |
| Nationality | British (English) |
| Born | (1983-01-07) 7 January 1983 (age 43) |
| Sport | |
| Sport | Lawn & indoor bowls |
| Achievements and titles | |
| Highest world ranking | **21** (November 2025)[[1]](#cite_note-1) |
| Medal record  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | Men's [bowls](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls") | | | | Representing  [England](/wiki/England "England") | | | | [World Indoor Bowls Championships](/wiki/World_Indoor_Bowls_Championships "World Indoor Bowls Championships") | | | | Gold medal – first place | [2018 Yarmouth](/wiki/2018_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2018 World Indoor Bowls Championship") | [Open singles](/wiki/2018_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2018 World Indoor Bowls Championship") | | Gold medal – first place | [2018 Yarmouth](/wiki/2018_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2018 World Indoor Bowls Championship") | [Open pairs](/wiki/2018_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2018 World Indoor Bowls Championship") | | Gold medal – first place | [2021 Yarmouth](/wiki/2021_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2021 World Indoor Bowls Championship") | [Open singles](/wiki/2021_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2021 World Indoor Bowls Championship") | | Gold medal – first place | [2021 Yarmouth](/wiki/2021_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2021 World Indoor Bowls Championship") | [Open pairs](/wiki/2021_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2021 World Indoor Bowls Championship") | | |

**Mark Dawes** (born 1983) is an English international [lawn and indoor bowler](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls") and a four times world indoor champion.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Bowls career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mark_Dawes&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Bowls career")]

He won the [Men's National Junior title](/wiki/List_of_Bowls_England_Men%27s_National_Championships "List of Bowls England Men's National Championships") in 2005 at the National Championships.[[3]](#cite_note-3) By 2014, he had reached a career high world indoor ranking of 8.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In 2018, he won the [World Indoor Open Pairs title](/wiki/2018_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2018 World Indoor Bowls Championship") with [Jamie Chestney](/wiki/Jamie_Chestney "Jamie Chestney").[[5]](#cite_note-5) and then followed this by taking the World Open Singles Championship, defeating [Robert Paxton](/wiki/Robert_Paxton_(bowls) "Robert Paxton (bowls)") in a hard fought final. Dawes had been the player of the tournament and Paxton did well to take the final to a tie break.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In 2020, his indoor club, Blackpool Newton Hall BC, permanently closed and Dawes was forced to find a new club.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Despite the loss of his club he won a third world indoor title when winning the open pairs at the [2021 World Indoor Bowls Championship](/wiki/2021_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2021 World Indoor Bowls Championship") for the second time with Jamie Chestney.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9) Dawes then reproduced his good form to win the singles event for the second time, defeating [Greg Harlow](/wiki/Greg_Harlow "Greg Harlow") in the final 10-3 11-5 and record a fourth world indoor title in total.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)
